conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Hello! I'd be happy to help you determine if you're eligible for Child Benefit. Let me first review the eligibility rules so I can ask you the right questions.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for waiting. To help determine your eligibility for Child Benefit, I need to ask you some questions.

First, do you have any children you're asking about?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Hello! Yes, I do. I have one child, Alex, who's 16 years old.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information. To assess your eligibility for Child Benefit for Alex, I need to ask:

Is Alex still in approved education or training (such as school, college, or an approved training course)?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, Alex is still in college doing A-levels.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
That's helpful, thank you. Since Alex is 16 and in approved education, that meets one of the key criteria.

Now, do you live with Alex in the UK?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, I do. We live together in Manchester.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Excellent. Just a few more questions to complete the assessment.

Is anyone else currently claiming Child Benefit for Alex?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, nobody else is claiming Child Benefit for Alex.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you. One final question to complete my assessment:

Do you or your partner have an individual income over £60,000 per year? (This wouldn't affect your eligibility, but there may be a tax charge to consider.)

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, neither of us earns over £60,000 a year.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Thank you for providing all that information. I now have everything I need to assess your eligibility for Child Benefit for Alex.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
**Good news! You are eligible for Child Benefit for Alex.**

Based on the information you've provided:

✅ Alex is 16 years old and in approved education (doing A-levels at college), which means you can continue to receive Child Benefit even though Alex is over 16

✅ You live with Alex in the UK (Manchester), which establishes that you're responsible for them

✅ No one else is claiming Child Benefit for Alex

✅ Neither you nor your partner earns over £60,000, so you won't need to worry about the High Income Child Benefit tax charge

You meet all the eligibility requirements for Child Benefit. If you haven't already claimed, you can apply online or by post. The Child Benefit will continue as long as Alex remains in approved education or training and is under 20 years old.

claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 1 child:
  - Alex is 16 years old and lives with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 16 and in approved education
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

expected_eligibility.name: Alex
expected_eligibility.reason: Claimant lives in the UK; Child is 16 and in approved education; Child lives with claimant; Child is not in local authority care; Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation; Child is not fostered; Child does not work 24+ hours/week; Child has not started an apprenticeship in England; Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right
circumstances: Child is 16 and in approved education
Child is not in local authority care
Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

reasonings: Alex is 16 years old and is in approved education (college doing A-levels). According to the eligibility rules, you normally qualify for Child Benefit for a child under 16, and this continues if the child is under 20 and stays in approved education or training. The claimant lives with Alex in the UK (Manchester), which satisfies the responsibility requirement. No one else is claiming Child Benefit for Alex, meeting the rule that only one person can claim Child Benefit for a child. Therefore, all eligibility criteria are met for Alex.
arguments.overall_reasoning: The claimant is eligible for Child Benefit for their 16-year-old child Alex. Alex is in approved education (A-levels at college), the claimant lives with Alex in the UK, and no one else is claiming for Alex. All eligibility requirements are satisfied. Additionally, neither the claimant nor their partner earns over £60,000, so there would be no High Income Child Benefit tax charge to consider.
